senses.definition: in a way that is very favorable or enthusiastic

examples.x: The CEO spoke about the company's future plans in glowing terms, highlighting their commitment to innovation and growth.
The novel was praised in glowing terms for its intricate plot, rich character development, and lyrical prose.
The renowned pianist was described in glowing terms for her virtuosic skills and captivating performances.

senses.definition: to make someone tempted to do or try something

examples.x: When I saw the display of gourmet chocolates, it was enough to make my mouth water.
The menu at the new restaurant is so diverse that it's guaranteed to make your mouth water.
The thought of that juicy steak cooked to perfection makes my mouth water.

senses.definition: with a high level of energy or enthusiasm

examples.x: The ambitious entrepreneur launched their startup with both guns blazing, tackling challenges head-on and pursuing success with unwavering drive.
The fiery debater entered the debate with guns blazing, presenting compelling arguments and countering every opposing viewpoint with precision.
The passionate speaker took the stage with guns blazing, delivering a powerful and persuasive speech that captivated the audience.
The team entered the final match with all guns blazing, displaying an impressive display of skill and determination to secure the championship.

senses.definition: feeling very happy and excited about something

examples.x: She was thrilled to pieces when her favorite band announced a surprise concert in her city.
The children were thrilled to death when they found out they were going to Disneyland for their summer vacation.
When she received the scholarship, she was thrilled to death and couldn't contain her excitement.
Winning the championship was a dream come true, and he was thrilled to death to be pieces the trophy.

senses.definition: to make someone become really amazed

examples.x: The stunning view of the mountains took her breath away.
As she stepped outside, the beauty of the sunset took her breath away.
The elegance of her wedding dress took his breath away the moment he saw her.

word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌnz hˈɑːɹt skˈɪp mˈɪs ɐ bˈiːt

senses.definition: used to describe to a person feels a sudden, strong emotional reaction or physical sensation, often due to excitement, surprise, fear, or attraction

examples.x: When she saw the surprise proposal, her heart skipped a beat from the excitement.
The unexpected phone call from her long-lost friend made her heart skip a beat with joy.
As he approached the finish line in first place, his heart skipped a beat in anticipation of victory.
The eerie noise in the dark forest made her heart skip a beat out of fear.
When he saw his childhood crush after many years, his heart skipped a beat with nostalgia and longing.

senses.definition: to feel really happy and fulfilled

examples.x: The team erupted in cheers and jumped for joy as they scored the winning goal in the final seconds of the game.
Upon hearing the news of her sister's engagement, she couldn't help but jump for joy and shower her with congratulations.
When she received the acceptance letter from her dream university, she jumped for joy, unable to contain her excitement.

senses.definition: to become extremely excited or enthusiastic about something

examples.x: After weeks of anticipation, she finally received her long-awaited promotion and went ape, celebrating with a dance of sheer delight.
The kids went ape when they saw the ice cream truck coming down the street, running and shouting with pure joy.
When the band started playing their favorite song, the crowd went ape, dancing and cheering with uncontainable excitement.

word_list.word: like a kid with a new toy
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪk ɐ kˈɪd wɪð ɐ nˈuː tˈɔɪ

senses.definition: very thrilled or excited about something new that one has got

examples.x: The child unwrapped the shiny new bicycle and couldn't wait to hop on and ride, grinning from ear to ear like a kid with a new toy.
When the virtual reality headset arrived, he eagerly put it on and started exploring the virtual world like a kid with a new toy.

word_list.word: like a kid in a (candy store|toy shop)
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪk ɐ kˈɪd ɪn ɐ kˈændi stˈoːɹ ɔːɹ tˈɔɪ ʃˈɑːp

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who is having a really good time and cannot control their excitement

examples.x: As soon as she entered the bookstore, her eyes widened with excitement, and she wandered through the aisles like a kid in a candy store, eager to explore the vast collection of books.

When the tech enthusiast stepped into the electronics expo, he couldn't contain his excitement, darting from booth to booth like a kid in a candy store, marveling at the latest gadgets and innovations.

word_list.pronunciation: ˈɔːl wˈʌnz kɹˈɪsməsᵻz hæv kˈʌm ɐtwˈʌns

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who is extremely pleased with their luck

word_list.pronunciation: bˈoːɹd ˌaʊɾəv wˈʌnz mˈaɪnd bɹˈeɪn skˈʌl

senses.definition: to be bored to an extreme degree

examples.x: As I waited for my delayed flight with no entertainment or books, I was bored out of my mind at the airport.
Sitting through that three-hour lecture on a topic I had no interest in left me bored out of my skull.
Spending the entire weekend doing repetitive household chores left me feeling bored out of my mind.
The monotonous office job with no challenging tasks made me bored out of my brain every day.
When the power went out and there was nothing to do, I found myself bored out of my mind without any form of entertainment.

word_list.pronunciation: tʃˈɑːmp tʃˈæmp tʃˈeɪf æt ðə bˈɪt

senses.definition: to hardly be able to wait for something due to intense excitement

examples.x: After two hours of waiting in the airport lobby, we were chafing at the bit to finally get on the plane.
As soon as the kids saw the pool, they were chafing at the bit to get in.
Biden chomps at the bit for his turn at debate.
Harry wants more power and position at court than Edward is willing to give him and he chafes at the bit, which only exacerbates his dilema.
I expect you're champing at the bit, so we'll get things going as soon as we can.

senses.definition: a state in which little bumps appear on the skin and bodily hair becomes upright because of excitement, cold, or fear

examples.x: As the roller coaster reached its highest peak, a surge of excitement ran through me, and I got goose bumps.
The haunting melody gave me goose bumps as it resonated through the concert hall.
When he proposed to her, she felt a rush of emotions, and goose bumps appeared on her skin.

senses.definition: used when one is attentively watching or listening to someone or something with great interest or excitement

examples.x: As the tightrope walker inched across the high wire, the spectators were on the edge of their seats, holding their breath in nervous anticipation.
During the gripping courtroom drama, the jurors were on the edge of their seats, waiting for the verdict to be announced.
The intense basketball game had the entire crowd on the edge of their seats, anxiously awaiting the last-second shot.
The thrilling roller coaster ride had me on the edge of my seat, my heart pounding with excitement.

senses.definition: to be really excited for something that is about to happen

examples.x: As the roller coaster climbed to its peak, the thrill-seekers smacked their lips in excitement for the exhilarating drop that awaited them.
He was licking his lips looking at all those fancy sports cars.
Property developers have been smacking their lips at the thoughts of getting their hands on such prime real estate.

senses.definition: in a way that shows one's intense enthusiasm

examples.x: Count me in for the charity event," he said with a smile, assuring his commitment to the cause with bells on.
I'll be there with bells on!"
When her favorite band announced a reunion tour, she immediately bought tickets and declared, "I'll be at the front row with bells on!"

senses.definition: to be full of energy and excitement

examples.x: The caffeine had kicked in, and he was bouncing off the walls with energy, unable to sit still.
The kids were so hyped up after the birthday party that they were bouncing off the walls for hours.
The news of her acceptance into her dream college had her bouncing off the walls with joy.
Trying to keep up with the energetic toddlers felt like they were bouncing off the walls all day long.

senses.definition: full of energy and joy

examples.x: Despite the early morning start, she arrived at work bright-eyed and bushy-tailed, ready to tackle the day with enthusiasm.
Despite the rainy weather, the marathon runners arrived at the starting line bright-eyed and bushy-tailed, determined to give their best performance.
The children woke up on Christmas morning, bright-eyed and bushy-tailed, eager to open their presents.
The team members gathered for the meeting, all bright-eyed and bushy-tailed, eager to brainstorm new ideas.

senses.definition: used to say that one can do whatever one desires
to surprise or impress someone greatly

examples.x: The singer's voice knocked me off my feet.
The news of their victory knocked everyone off their feet.
The painting knocked me off my feet with its beauty.

senses.definition: to appeal to someone's particular interests or preferences

examples.x: The idea of traveling to exotic locations really tickles her fancy.
The prospect of trying out new recipes in the kitchen always tickles her fancy.
The quirky sense of humor in the movie tickled his fancy, and he couldn't stop laughing.
The idea of learning a new language has always tickled her fancy.
He's a history buff, so the museum's collection of artifacts really tickled his fancy.
